behaviors.description: Cache keys generated from identical query parameters are identical, and keys from different parameters do not collide (for tested cases).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is key-derivation logic in one component (MOCK-MISS: no required module handoff). No OS/network/subprocess dependence (REAL-INFRA: no). No AI output dependence (LLM-DEP: no). Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text is only about deterministic key generation from query parameters, not cross-module propagation.
behaviors.test_description: Call the key-generator function directly with canonicalized parameter sets: same params in different input orders produce the same key; known distinct params produce distinct keys; include edge cases (nulls, arrays, numeric/string forms) to catch normalization bugs.

behaviors.description: TTL is computed correctly from each data category freshness rule (for example, profiles=5m, catalog=1h).
behaviors.justification: This behavior is about calculator + category-rule configuration wiring (MOCK-MISS: bug can appear at module boundary if category-to-rule handoff is wrong). Integration brake: (a) no real OS/network/subprocess required; (b) no AI output; (c) no multi-agentic chain; (d) no LLM stage orchestration. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text only says configured freshness rule, not real infrastructure timing/network/DB interaction.
behaviors.test_description: Use real in-process config loader/module plus TTL service: load category freshness config, request TTL for multiple categories, and assert returned TTL durations match config values and defaults/fallback paths.

behaviors.description: When memory usage crosses threshold, eviction strategy switches from LRU to LFU per configuration.
behaviors.justification: Failure is in interaction between memory-pressure source, threshold config, and strategy selector (MOCK-MISS: cross-module handoff/state boundary). Integration brake: (a) no real OS requirement if memory metric provider is an in-process module/fake; (b) no AI dependence; (c) no multi-agentic sequence; (d) no LLM orchestration. Rejection gate: rejected System because behavior text requires threshold-based switching logic, not real OS memory primitives.
behaviors.test_description: Wire strategy selector with real config module and a controllable memory-metrics provider; drive usage below/above threshold and assert active policy transitions LRU->LFU exactly at configured boundary, including hysteresis/edge equality if specified.

behaviors.description: A write to PostgreSQL invalidates all cache entries whose keys match affected rows.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly depends on real PostgreSQL write semantics and cache invalidation trigger path (REAL-INFRA: yes, DB process/external API boundary). Mocks can hide failures in trigger/listener/sql mapping (MOCK-HIDE: yes). Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text says 'write to underlying PostgreSQL table' and requires observing real DB-driven invalidation behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run against a real PostgreSQL instance and real cache layer: seed cache entries for target rows, execute UPDATE/INSERT/DELETE on the table, then assert all matching keys are invalidated while non-matching keys remain.

behaviors.description: Eviction removes correct entries: LRU under LRU mode and LFU under LFU mode when cache is full.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is eviction algorithm behavior within cache policy implementation; no required external handoff (MOCK-MISS: low). No OS/network/subprocess requirement (REAL-INFRA: no), no AI dependence, no multi-step agent pipeline. Rejection gate: rejected Integration because behavior text focuses on policy correctness at full-capacity condition, which can be exercised directly in-memory.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate cache policy object with fixed capacity; for LRU scenario, perform access sequence and assert least-recently-used key is evicted on insert overflow; for LFU, assert least-frequently-used (with tie-break rule) is evicted.
